title: Controlled Hormuz
summary:
Main
By Eirini Diamantara & Dimitris Roumeliotis
The Strait of Hormuz is no longer simply a chokepoint. It is gradually being reshaped into a controlled corridor where access is conditional, selective, and increasingly political. What began as a wartime disruption is now evolving into a structural shift, with Iran attempting to formalise its influence over one of the most critical arteries of global energy trade.
